raw_output:
```
══════════════════════════════════════════════
  CODE REVIEW REPORT
  Files changed: 2 | Lines added: +90 | Lines removed: +0
══════════════════════════════════════════════

── BLOCKERS (1) ────────────────────────────

[B1] CartStore.ts:13 & UserStore.ts:14 — Circular dependency between CartStore and UserStore
     CartStore instantiates UserStore in its constructor, and UserStore instantiates
     CartStore in its constructor. This creates a circular module dependency that will
     cause one of the classes to receive an undefined reference when the modules are
     first loaded, leading to runtime errors.
     Fix: Use dependency injection. Pass the required store instance via constructor
     parameters, or use a singleton pattern with lazy initialization. Example:
     ```
     constructor(userStore: UserStore) {
       this.userStore = userStore;
     }
     ```

── WARNINGS (4) ────────────────────────────

[W1] UserStore.ts:17-18 — No error handling for async login operation
     If authApi.login() throws or rejects, the error will propagate uncaught and
     currentUser will remain null, leaving the user in an inconsistent state.
     Suggestion: Wrap in try-catch or let the caller handle errors explicitly.

[W2] CartStore.ts:38 — localStorage usage without availability check
     localStorage.setItem() will throw in SSR environments, privacy mode browsers,
     or when storage quota is exceeded. This will crash cart operations.
     Suggestion: Wrap in try-catch or check for localStorage availability first.

[W3] CartStore.ts:16-22 — No validation for item quantity or price
     addItem() accepts item.quantity and item.price without validation. Negative
     quantities or prices would corrupt cart totals and create confusing UX.
     Suggestion: Add validation: `if (item.quantity <= 0 || item.price < 0) throw new Error(...)`

[W4] No test coverage for new business logic
     CartStore and UserStore contain critical e-commerce logic (cart totals,
     persistence, authentication state) but no tests are included in this diff.
     Suggestion: Add unit tests covering addItem, removeItem, getTotal, login, logout,
     and edge cases (empty cart, duplicate items, missing userId).

── VERDICT ─────────────────────────────────

  ✗ NOT READY — 1 blocker(s) must be resolved before merging.

══════════════════════════════════════════════
```

```
── ORCHESTRATOR SUMMARY ────────────────────────
{"blockers": 1, "warnings": 4, "suggestions": 0, "verdict": "NOT_READY"}
────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

blockers.title: CartStore.ts:13 & UserStore.ts:14 — Circular dependency between CartStore and UserStore
blockers.body:
CartStore instantiates UserStore in its constructor, and UserStore instantiates
CartStore in its constructor. This creates a circular module dependency that will
cause one of the classes to receive an undefined reference when the modules are
first loaded, leading to runtime errors.
Fix: Use dependency injection. Pass the required store instance via constructor
parameters, or use a singleton pattern with lazy initialization. Example:
```
constructor(userStore: UserStore) {
this.userStore = userStore;
}
```

     first loaded, leading to runtime errors.
     Fix: Use dependency injection. Pass the required store instance via constructor
     parameters, or use a singleton pattern with lazy initialization. Example:
